key_points: The application deadline for the Multifamily Housing Incentive Program has been extended to February 28, 2027, and seven new projects were added to the eligible list.
Rental Inclusionary In-Lieu Fees are reduced to $0 for eligible projects that provide at least 5% of units on-site at up to 110% Area Median Income (AMI).

Construction taxes for eligible Multifamily Housing Incentive projects are reduced by 50% for the first 3,600 units and 25% for additional units, provided building permits are obtained by February 28, 2027.

The Downtown Residential High-Rise Incentive Program was renamed to the Downtown Residential Incentive Program and expanded to include commercial-to-residential conversions for mid- and high-rise buildings.
Amendments to the Mobilehome Rent Ordinance and Inclusionary Housing regulations are proposed for substantive updates and cleanup.
analysis.summary: San Jose is implementing significant financial incentives to stimulate multifamily housing development and downtown commercial-to-residential conversions by extending program application deadlines, waiving inclusionary fees for projects with specific affordability components, and reducing construction taxes for eligible developments.

attachment_content_probes.content_excerpt: on, and Development Intergovernmental Transfer Grant Funding for California Advancing and Innovating Medi-Cal Readiness and Implementation RECOMMENDATION (a ) Adopt a resolution authorizing the Housing Director, or his designee, to: (1 ) Accept the Providing Access and Transforming Health Capacity and Infrastructure Transition, Expansion, and Development Intergovernmental Transfer (PATH CITED) grant in the amount of $1,323,036 in federal funding to support California Advancing and Innovating Medi-Cal readiness and implementation within the City’s homelessness response system; and (2 ) Commit the required City match of $1,323,037, consistent with grant requirements, to support these California Advancing and Innovating Medi-Cal readiness and implementation activities, including workflow development, billing readiness, and coordination across Emergency Interim Housing operations. (b ) Adopt the following 2025-2026 Appropriation Ordinance and Funding Sources Resolution amendments in the Multi-Source Housing Fund: (1 ) Increase the estimate for Revenue from Federal Government by $600,000; (2) Establish the PATH CITED Grant appropriation to the Housing Department in the amount of $1,200,000; and (3) Decrease the Ending Fund Balance by $600,000. top_results.snippet: Skip to page body  Home  Residents  Businesses  Jobs  Your Government  News & Stories. # City of San José. facebook   twitter   instagram   linkedin   youtube   Next Door Home. Your Government » Appointees » City Clerk » City Council Agendas & Minutes. # City Council Meeting Agendas and Minutes. *\*\*\* Send any correspondence related to Council items that you wish Council to see prior to the meeting to city.clerk@sanjoseca.gov or Office of the City Clerk, City of San José, 200 E Santa Clara Street, San José, CA 95113. Items must be received by 8:00 a.m. on the day of the meeting to ensure it can be distributed prior to the meeting. Items received after the meeting begins will be posted online after the meeting. Hard copies may be brought directly to the meeting.*. * Council Agendas 2019 to Present - Select from the Date Dropdown Menu. * Archived Council Minutes 1950-2011 (Microfilm). * To obtain Council Agendas, Minutes and Memos 1995 – 2011 please email city.clerk@sanjoseca.gov.
